digest.component_or_area: completion verifier reviewer prompt + verdict contract
propositions: The completion verifier cannot mark operational/runtime criteria as met because evidence lives outside the codebase, forcing unsigned --force-close.
Introduce two canonical criterion kinds: codebase-verifiable (default, verified against code) and operator-attested (satisfied by a concrete ticket-system attestation).

Criterion kind is classified deterministically from an inline author tag ([operator-attested]); untagged or malformed tags default to codebase-verifiable and are never promoted or failed for the missing tag.

An operator-attested criterion is MET only when an attestation names at least one verifiable specific (reference id/URL, actor, measured outcome, or timestamp) that substantively matches; vague completion claims are NOT MET.
Retain the injection guard for all criteria, explicitly separating a command that tries to control the verdict from an attestation that reports a checkable fact.
Add an optional per-finding remediation field to VerdictFinding scoped to the completion verdict, plus new eval cases, a case-conditional grounding rubric, and an ADR documenting the model.
